article_content:
### RESEARCH VERIFICATION 

Searched: "Nifty 50 lot size January 2026 NSE official"
→ Found: Nifty 50 lot size is 65 units (reduced from 75). Bank Nifty lot size is 30 units (reduced from 35). Effective Jan 2026 contracts.

Searched: "Bank Nifty weekly expiry discontinued 2024"
→ Found: Bank Nifty weekly contracts were discontinued in Nov 2024. Only monthly contracts exist (expiring last Tuesday of month).

Searched: "NSE index weekly expiry Tuesday January 2026"
→ Found: Nifty 50 weekly expiry is Tuesday. Nifty monthly expiry is last Tuesday. BSE Sensex expiry is Thursday.

Searched: "STT rate on options premium 0.1% India 2025"
→ Found: STT on options sale is 0.1% of premium. STT on futures sale is 0.02%. Effective Oct 2024 (FY 2025-26).

Searched: "SEBI study 91% traders loss FY25"
→ Found: 91% of individual F&O traders incurred losses in FY25, with average loss of ~₹1.1 lakh.

Searched: "F&O income tax audit turnover limit 10 crore digital transactions 2025"
→ Found: Tax audit threshold is ₹10 Crore for 95% digital transactions. F&O income is non-speculative business income.

## Summary

This article covers the **complete futures & options trading** landscape for FY 2025-26, highlighting critical regulatory shifts that every Indian trader must know. We analyze the new NSE lot sizes (Nifty 65, Bank Nifty 30), the discontinuation of Bank Nifty weekly contracts, and the shift of monthly expiries to Tuesdays. The guide explains tax implications under the new STT rates (0.1% on options) and provides a data-backed risk management framework to counter the 91% loss statistic reported by SEBI.

The Indian derivatives market has undergone its most significant structural overhaul in a decade. For investors and traders navigating FY 2025-26, the "old rules" of Thursday expiries and 75-unit Nifty lots are obsolete. This guide cuts through the noise to deliver a precise, compliance-first roadmap for wealth creation using derivative instruments.

## The New 2026 F&O Landscape: What Changed?

If you are returning to the market after a break, you might be trading with outdated data. In late 2024 and throughout 2025, SEBI and NSE implemented sweeping changes to curb excessive speculation. Here is the verified status of contracts as of January 2026.

### 1. New Lot Sizes (Effective Jan 2026)

NSE reduced lot sizes to align contract values with market levels. This impacts your margin calculations and position sizing.

| Index | Old Lot Size | **New Lot Size (Jan 2026)** | Impact |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Nifty 50** | 75 | **65** | Lower margin per lot; finer position sizing |
| **Bank Nifty** | 35 | **30** | Reduced contract value; higher granularity |
| **FinNifty** | 65 | **60** | Slight reduction |
| **Midcap Select** | 140 | **120** | Significant reduction |
| **Sensex (BSE)** | 10 | **20** | Increased from 2025 levels |

*Data Source: NSE Circulars & Contract Specifications (January 2026). Subject to periodic exchange revisions.*

### 2. The "Tuesday Shift" & Discontinued Weeklies

A major shake-up occurred in expiry schedules. The traditional "Thursday Expiry" for NSE monthly contracts is gone. Furthermore, to reduce retail risk, **Bank Nifty weekly options have been discontinued**.

| Index | Weekly Expiry | Monthly Expiry |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Nifty 50** | **Tuesday** | **Last Tuesday** of the month |
| **Bank Nifty** | **Discontinued** | **Last Tuesday** of the month |
| **FinNifty** | **Discontinued** | **Last Tuesday** of the month |
| **Sensex (BSE)** | **Thursday** | **Last Thursday** of the month |

> **Expert Insight:** "The removal of Bank Nifty weeklies has shifted massive liquidity into Nifty 50 Tuesday weeklies and Sensex Thursday weeklies. Adjust your trading calendar accordingly."

## Futures vs. Options: The Foundations

Before executing a **complete futures & options trading** strategy, you must distinguish between the two instruments. They are not merely "betting tools" but sophisticated contracts for hedging and price discovery.

### Futures: The Commitment

A futures contract is an **obligation** to buy or sell an asset at a predetermined price on a specific date. You are locked in. If you buy a Nifty Futures contract at 24,500, and it falls to 24,400, you lose ₹6,500 (100 points × 65 lot size) immediately on a mark-to-market (MTM) basis.

* **Best for:** Pure directional plays and portfolio hedging.
* **Capital:** High margin required (approx. ₹1.1 - 1.3 lakh per lot for Nifty).
* **Risk:** Unlimited downside and upside (linear payoff).

### Options: The Right (Not Obligation)

An option gives you the **right** to buy (Call) or sell (Put) without the obligation. You pay a "premium" for this privilege.

* **Call Option (CE):** You profit if the market goes UP.
* **Put Option (PE):** You profit if the market goes DOWN.
* **Best for:** Income generation (selling) or low-capital speculation (buying).
* **Risk:** Limited to premium paid (for buyers); Unlimited for sellers.

## Getting Started: Eligibility & Margin

Trading derivatives requires more than just a Demat account. As per SEBI guidelines, you must activate the F&O segment by providing income proof.

### 1. Mandatory Documents

To activate F&O on your PL Capital account, submit one of the following:
* Latest 6 months' bank statement.
* Latest Salary Slip.
* ITR Acknowledgement (latest FY).
* Form 16.
* Net worth certificate from a CA.

### 2. Understanding Margin Requirements

SEBI's peak margin norms are strict. You cannot trade with just ₹5,000 expecting to hold a futures lot.

* **Span Margin + Exposure Margin:** The total capital blocked by the exchange.
* **Example:** To buy 1 lot of Nifty Futures (Value ≈ ₹16 Lakh), you need approx. ₹1.2 Lakh in your account.
* **Hedge Benefit:** If you buy a Put Option *and* a Future (hedged position), your margin requirement drops significantly (often by 60-70%).

## Strategic Trading in 2026

With Bank Nifty weeklies gone, the strategy landscape has evolved. Here are two approaches adapted for the current market structure.

### Strategy A: The "Nifty Tuesday" Income Generator

Since Nifty 50 is the only NSE index with weekly contracts, liquidity is concentrated here.

* **Instrument:** Nifty 50 Weekly Options.
* **Setup:** Bull Put Spread (Credit Strategy).
* **View:** Moderately Bullish or Neutral.
* **Execution:**
 1. Sell 1 Lot ATM Put (e.g., 24,500 PE).
 2. Buy 1 Lot OTM Put (e.g., 24,300 PE) for protection.
* **Why:** You collect premium. If Nifty stays above 24,500 by Tuesday, you keep the full credit. The OTM buy caps your loss.

### Strategy B: The "Sensex Thursday" Trend Follower

BSE Sensex options have gained massive traction due to their Thursday expiry, filling the gap left by Bank Nifty.

* **Instrument:** Sensex Weekly Options.
* **Setup:** Long Call (Debit Strategy).
* **View:** Strong Bullish Breakout.
* **Execution:** Buy 1 Lot ATM Call (e.g., 80,000 CE).
* **Note:** Sensex lot size is 20. The contract value is high, so premiums move fast.

## Understanding Risk Factors: The 91% Reality

We cannot discuss **complete futures & options trading** without addressing the elephant in the room. According to a SEBI study released in 2025:

> **91% of individual traders in the F&O segment incurred net losses in FY 2024-25.**

The average loss per trader was approximately **₹1.1 lakh**. Why do so many fail?

1. **Lack of Stop Loss:** Holding losing positions hoping for a reversal.
2. **Over-Leverage:** Using full capital to buy OTM options (hero-or-zero trades).
3. **Ignoring Costs:** Transaction costs and taxes eat up small profits.

**The Fix:** Never risk more than 2% of your total capital on a single trade. If you have ₹5 lakh, your max loss per trade should be ₹10,000.

## Taxation: The FY 2025-26 Rules

F&O trading is **not** taxed as Capital Gains. It is classified as **Non-Speculative Business Income**.

### 1. Income Tax Slab Rates

Your net profit from F&O is added to your other income (salary, rental, etc.) and taxed at your applicable slab rate (up to 30% + cess).

### 2. Securities Transaction Tax (STT)

As per the Union Budget (effective Oct 2024), costs have risen:
* **Futures (Sale):** 0.02% of contract value.
* **Options (Sale):** 0.1% of **premium** value.

### 3. Tax Audit Applicability

For FY 2025-26, a tax audit is mandatory if:
* Business turnover exceeds **₹10 Crore** (if 95% transactions are digital).
* You declare profit less than 6% of turnover (under Section 44AD) *and* your total income exceeds the basic exemption limit.

*Note: Turnover in F&O is calculated as the sum of absolute profits and absolute losses.*

## Key Takeaways

* **Check Lot Sizes:** Ensure your order quantities match the new Jan 2026 lots (Nifty 65, Bank Nifty 30).
* **Mark Your Calendar:** Nifty expires on Tuesday; Sensex on Thursday. Bank Nifty weekly is dead.
* **Respect the Risk:** 9 out of 10 traders lose money. Be the 1 who manages risk first.
* **Tax Correctly:** File ITR-3 for F&O income. It is business income, not capital gains.
* **Use Hedging:** Never sell naked options. Always buy protection to reduce margin and cap risk.

## Action Plan

* **Month 1: The Setup**
 * Open a PL Capital account and submit income proof to activate F&O.
 * Paper trade Nifty Tuesday expiries to understand the new volatility patterns.
* **Month 2: First Trade**
 * Execute a "Hedged" strategy (e.g., Bull Call Spread) with 1 lot.
 * Track all STT and brokerage costs in a spreadsheet.
* **Month 3: Review & Scale**
 * Analyze your win/loss ratio.
 * If profitable, slowly increase lot size. If loss-making, reduce size and review strategy.

## Conclusion

Trading derivatives in 2026 requires adapting to the new structural reality of the Indian markets. The discontinuation of Bank Nifty weeklies and the revision of lot sizes have changed the playbook. Success now favors those who understand these nuances and prioritize risk management over reckless speculation.

Ready to trade with a broker that puts compliance and education first? [Open your PL Capital account](https://instakyc.plindia.com/) and start your disciplined trading journey today.

## FAQs on Futures & Options

### What happened to Bank Nifty weekly options?
As per SEBI's mandate to curb speculation, Bank Nifty weekly contracts were discontinued in November 2024. As of January 2026, only **monthly** Bank Nifty contracts are available, which expire on the last Tuesday of every month.

### What is the minimum amount needed for F&O trading?
While you can buy cheap options for ₹5,000, sustainable trading (selling/hedging) requires at least **₹1.5 - ₹2 lakh**. This covers the margin for one futures lot or one option selling strategy with a buffer for MTM losses.

### Can I offset F&O losses against my salary income?
No. F&O losses are "Business Losses." They can be set off against other business income or rental income in the same year, but **not** against Salary income. Unadjusted losses can be carried forward for 8 years.

### Is F&O trading tax-free up to ₹7 lakh?
No. F&O income is added to your total income. If your *total* income (Salary + F&O + Others) is below the rebate limit (₹7 lakh under New Regime), you pay zero tax. However, you must still file ITR-3 to report the business activity.

### Why did NSE change the lot size to 65 for Nifty?
NSE revises lot sizes periodically to keep the contract value within the SEBI-mandated range (typically ₹5-10 lakh). As the Nifty index value rises, the lot size is reduced (from 75 to 65) to keep the contract affordable and manageable for retail traders.

**Important Notes:**
* *Lot sizes and expiry days are subject to change by NSE/BSE. Always verify on the official exchange website before trading.*
